title: Decouple project open/add from a blocking remote git request (board worktree mus…
prompt:
GOAL (root-cause, confirmed by reading the code): Opening/adding a project must NOT be gated on a reachable git remote. Today the board-branch-decoupling startup path makes a BLOCKING 'git fetch origin <board-branch>' and, when that fetch fails for a NETWORK reason, throws and refuses — so the project never loads, is invisible in the UI, and the failure RETRIES every ~30s forever. Usability of a project is wrongly coupled to origin reachability. Decouple them: the project must always open and be usable locally; remote fetch/push must be BEST-EFFORT, non-blocking, and only surface a status hint when it can't reach origin.

PRECISE CODE FINDING (so you don't repeat a wrong assumption):
In src/workspace/board-worktree.ts, createBoardWorktree(repoPath, worktreePath, branch):
- It ALREADY handles the local-branch case first:  — so 'local branch exists but fetch fails' is NOT a real bug path; do not waste effort there.
- The throw is reached ONLY when there is NO local board branch AND a remote is configured AND the fetch failed for a NON-missing-ref reason:
    const fetchResult = await runGit(repoPath, ['fetch', remote, branch], { timeoutMs: BOARD_NETWORK_GIT_TIMEOUT_MS });
    if (await remoteBranchExists(...)) { worktree add --track; return; }
    if (!fetchResult.ok && !isMissingRemoteRefError(fetchResult.output)) {
      throw new Error('Could not fetch the board branch ... Refusing to initialize an empty board over data that may live on the remote.');
    }
- i.e. the FIELD trigger is: no local kanban/board branch + origin unreachable/timed-out/auth-failed (NOT 'branch missing on remote' — that case already degrades to creating an empty orphan branch via createOrphanBranchViaPlumbing).
- The retry loop / caller is ensureBoardWorktree → setupBoardWorktree, invoked from the workspace-state load + the migrateDecoupleBoardToBranch path in prepareRepoRuntimeHome; setup failure is logged ERROR and retried (~30s) by the load path.

WHY THE CURRENT BEHAVIOR EXISTS (preserve the intent, change the consequence): the refuse-to-init-empty guard avoids creating an empty local board that would SHADOW real board data living only on an (unreachable) remote — a legitimate concern for a cold clone. The bug is that it makes this a HARD, PROJECT-HIDING, infinitely-retrying failure instead of a graceful, non-blocking degraded state.

REQUIRED CHANGE (chosen direction: local-first + remote sync degraded to non-blocking background):
1. createBoardWorktree / setupBoardWorktree must NEVER throw-and-hide the project because origin is unreachable. The project must open and the board be usable from LOCAL git state regardless of remote reachability.
2. When there is no local board branch AND the remote can't be reached: do NOT block startup. Instead enter a clearly-labeled degraded state — keep the project visible/usable (read/operate on the local board, e.g. fall back to the pre-decoupling code-branch data OR a local orphan board), and mark board sync status as 'remote unreachable / will retry' rather than erroring out. Crucially, retries must be BACKGROUND and rate-limited (no 30s ERROR-log spam; back off).
3. Preserve the anti-clobber safety WITHOUT hiding the project: if remote data might exist but is currently unreachable, the degraded state must avoid PUSHING a divergent empty board over it (so we never overwrite remote data) — but it must still let the user work locally. Once origin becomes reachable, a background fetch reconciles (honor the existing conflict-surfacing: merge --abort on real conflict, never auto-resolve — see memory 'board-branch-decoupling-p5-implemented').
4. Adding a BRAND-NEW project (no board data anywhere, possibly no/unreachable remote) must do ZERO blocking remote requests on the open path — initialize the local board immediately.
5. Surface the degraded/unreachable state through the existing board sync status channel (RuntimeBoardSyncStatus / board_sync_status_updated broadcast + the top-bar badge / BoardConflictDialog), so the user sees 'remote unreachable, working locally, retrying' instead of a vanished project.

WHERE TO LOOK:
- src/workspace/board-worktree.ts (createBoardWorktree, setupBoardWorktree, ensureBoardWorktree, getDefaultRemote, remoteBranchExists, isMissingRemoteRefError).
- src/workspace/board-sync.ts (the sync service + status states; retry/backoff; status enum already has a 'no remote configured' state and an 'error' state — reuse/extend rather than invent).
- The load/migration callers: prepareRepoRuntimeHome / migrateDecoupleBoardToBranch and the workspace-state load that retries setup; src/state/board-ref.ts.
- Status wire contract: RuntimeBoardSyncStatus in src/core/api-contract.ts; the badge in web-ui (components/home-board-sync-control.tsx) + BoardConflictDialog.

ACCEPTANCE:
- With NO local board branch and origin UNREACHABLE (offline / bad creds / timeout): the project OPENS and is usable on the local board; NO infinite 30s ERROR spam (background, backed-off retry only); board sync status shows a clear 'remote unreachable / retrying' state.
- The empty-board over remote-data clobber is still prevented (no divergent empty board is PUSHED over possibly-existing remote data while the remote is unreachable).
- Once origin becomes reachable again, a background sync reconciles local <-> remote (fast-forward / merge), surfacing genuine conflicts via the existing non-auto-resolve path.
- Brand-new project add performs no blocking remote git request on the open path.
- Unit/integration tests: (a) no local branch + unreachable remote -> project usable locally, status=degraded, no throw; (b) remote becomes reachable -> reconciles; (c) brand-new project -> local init, zero remote calls on open.
- typecheck + biome/lint clean; no 'any'; reuse existing status enums/types.
